rag_rules: Place output range guarantees in component type scopes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use AGREE logical operators and valid expression types in guarantees and assumptions.
Generate syntactically valid AGREE annex blocks with proper scoping and type consistency.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should use visible ports and constants, not implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid, visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Equation declarations should match the types of the expressions they define.
AGREE annexes must be placed in appropriate AADL scopes (type vs implementation).
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` with correct syntax and valid identifiers.
Ensure all expressions use valid AGREE types (int, real, bool) and visible model features.
Write stateful equations with initialized temporal recurrence and branching logic with nested if-then-else.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties like Deadline or Period as AGREE variables.
Do not invent new identifiers or types not present in the visible AADL scope.
Do not encode Boolean data ranges using numeric comparisons unless the port is explicitly typed as bool.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing parameters
Model fusion and insertion location decisions
